ending: Underlord ends with Lindon reaching the Underlord stage at last, a threshold once unthinkable for a former Unsouled and one that establishes him as a genuine rising power. The long feud with Jai Daishou and the Jai clan is brought to a head and settled through Eithan's decisive intervention, which finally exposes how formidable Lindon's eccentric patron truly is, along with hints of a far larger past. The companions have grown notably stronger together, with Yerin, Orthos, Dross, and Little Blue all at Lindon's side. Most consequentially, the events of the book and their rising strength have drawn the direct attention of the powerful Akura clan and its Monarch, chiefly through Lindon's new friend Akura Mercy, opening a path toward a stage of great clans and Monarchs far above the Blackflame Empire. The book closes with Lindon an Underlord, his circle poised to step onto that wider stage, and both his ambitions and his dangers scaling up to match his new standing.
in_short: Reunited and far stronger after Ghostwater, Lindon sets out to reach the Underlord stage, the threshold that separates true powers from ordinary sacred artists. As he trains for the difficult, personal advancement, aided by the mind-spirit Dross and his unusual arts, old enmities resurface: the Jai clan patriarch Jai Daishou moves against Eithan and the Arelius family in a long-running feud. At the same time, the companions come into contact with the Akura clan, one of the great Monarch-ruled families, chiefly through the cheerful Akura Mercy, opening a path to powers far beyond anything they have known. The Jai conflict forces Eithan to reveal his true, formidable strength and hidden past, and amid the crisis Lindon completes his advancement to Underlord. The book ends with Lindon newly counted among real powers, the Jai feud resolved, and the Akura clan's attention drawing the companions toward a much larger stage.

recaps.text: Previously: Lindon rose from a scorned Unsouled in the doomed land of Sacred Valley, set on his path by the powerful Suriel, and traveled the wider world with the sword artist Yerin under the patronage of the eccentric, formidable Eithan Arelius. He learned soulsmithing and the forbidden Path of Black Flame, bonded with the ancient tortoise Orthos, and defeated his rival Jai Long. When the dreadgod called the Bleeding Phoenix attacked the Blackflame Empire, Lindon and Yerin served with the Skysworn to endure it; the Phoenix was driven off but left the region devastated, and Yerin lost her bound blood-madra inheritance. Swept alone into the ancient, collapsing realm of Ghostwater, Lindon grew enormously, gained the mind-spirit Dross, and escaped far stronger than before. Now reunited and rapidly rising, Lindon and his companions set their sights on the Underlord stage, even as far greater powers begin to take notice of them.

Back among his companions and far stronger for his ordeal, Lindon commits himself to reaching the Underlord stage, a demanding threshold that marks the boundary between ordinary sacred artists and genuine powers. He trains and prepares for the difficult advancement, drawing on his unusual dual arts, Dross's sharpened mind, and everything he has gathered. At the same time, old enmities resurface as the Jai clan, led by the patriarch Jai Daishou, moves against Eithan and the Arelius family in a long-simmering feud. The rising tension threatens Serpent's Grave and the family that has sheltered Lindon, and it begins to force Eithan's carefully hidden strength into the open, entangling Lindon's personal pursuit of advancement with a larger conflict among established powers.

The world Lindon moves in widens dramatically as he and his companions come into contact with the Akura clan, one of the great families ruled by a Monarch, chiefly through the cheerful young Akura Mercy, who befriends them. The connection hints at powers far beyond anything they have dealt with and at a much larger stage opening ahead. Meanwhile the feud with Jai Daishou and the Jai clan deepens, bringing the threat of open violence to the Arelius family and pressing Eithan toward a direct confrontation. Lindon continues his difficult preparations for the Underlord advancement, aware that the threshold demands not just raw power but a personal breakthrough. The stakes climb on every front as personal ambition, an old blood feud, and the attention of far greater powers converge.

The conflict with the Jai clan comes to a head as Jai Daishou's schemes force an open confrontation, and Eithan is drawn into revealing more of his true power and hidden past than he ever has before, proving himself a far greater figure than his foolish manner suggested. Amid the danger to the Arelius family, Lindon reaches the crux of his own struggle to advance, undertaking the difficult and personal trial that the Underlord threshold requires. The two threads reinforce each other, with the crisis pushing Lindon and his companions to spend everything they have. The attention of the Akura clan and the exposure of Eithan's real stature make clear that the companions are being pulled onto a stage of Monarchs and great powers, far above the regional conflicts that once defined their struggles.

By the book's close, Lindon achieves his goal and advances to the Underlord stage, a milestone almost unthinkable for the former Unsouled and one that firmly establishes him as a rising power. The feud with Jai Daishou and the Jai clan is brought to a head and resolved through Eithan's decisive intervention, which lays bare just how formidable Lindon's patron truly is. Most significantly, the companions' growth and the events surrounding them have drawn the direct attention of the Akura clan and its Monarch, opening a path toward a far larger arena than the Blackflame Empire. Reunited, greatly strengthened, and newly counted among real powers, Lindon and his companions stand at the threshold of that wider stage. The book ends with Lindon an Underlord at last, his circle bound for the world of great clans and Monarchs, and the scale of his ambitions and his dangers rising to match his new strength.
